meaning: adj.无可争辩的,无可置疑的,不可否认的
examples: That they need more capital is incontestable.
"One thing is - I believe - incontestable," he writes.
The incontestable fact is that each of us is a completely unique store of inestimable value.
That Americans are entitled to freedom is incontestable on every rational principle.
It is simply incontestable that we need to do this, and we think that Congress will do it because they recognize the urgency and significance of this vote.
